fusion-problem-recognition.subject: {{ company }} — short-form content question
fusion-problem-recognition.body:
Hi {{ first_name }},

Quick question — what happens to your social media on weeks when you're flat out running {{ company }}?

Most restaurants we speak to post 1-2x a week when they mean to post daily. The algorithm punishes inconsistency, so reach drops, then engagement drops, then it's a slog to rebuild.

We set up a system for Chillingham Road's New Greek. They now have 2.6M views across TikTok/Instagram, 39K followers, all from content shot on an iPhone and edited same-day.

Worth a 15-minute call to show you what that looks like for {{ company }}?

Best,
Jay
Fusion Creative

fusion-social-proof.subject: Re: {{ company }} — results from similar client
fusion-social-proof.body:
{{ first_name }} — following up on my email last week.

We just wrapped a 60-day push for New Greek (Greek street food on Chillingham Road).

The numbers:
• 2.6M combined views
• 39K TikTok likes
• 178K Instagram accounts reached
• 4-5 videos per week, shot on iPhone, turned around same day

The difference: they stopped trying to be 'professional' and started posting what actually gets watched.

Happy to show you the exact format on a 15-minute call this week?

Jay

fusion-final-follow-up.subject: Last follow-up — {{ company }}
fusion-final-follow-up.body:
{{ first_name }},

Final follow-up from me.

If short-form content isn't the priority right now, I totally get it. Most restaurant owners are drowning in operations.

If it does become a priority in the next few months, here's my number: [PHONE]

Or reply here — either way, no hard sell, just wanted to check in one last time.

Jay
Fusion Creative
